findings.text: High-risk requests at or above the policy threshold now return "allow" instead of being denied, inverting the risk gate and weakening authorization.
Admin requests are now denied unconditionally, whereas admins previously bypassed owner approval and could be allowed.
The blocked_states denial check was removed, so a request in a blocked state can be allowed when owner_approved is true or owner approval is not required.
summarize_recent now selects limit + 1 items, causing an off-by-one result that exceeds the requested limit.
The new test only covers the unchanged owner-approved allow path and misses the changed risk threshold, admin denial, blocked state, and recent-summary limit behavior.
